title: Getting Started
excerpt: Get started with Static Website Generator and build your first Astro theme in minutes.
content:
# Getting Started

Welcome to Static Website Generator (SWG).

This guide will help you set up the Theme Starter, understand the project structure and build your first SWG theme.

## Requirements

Before you begin, make sure you have the following installed:

- Node.js 22 or later
- npm
- Git

## Clone the Theme Starter

Clone the repository to your local machine.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/staticwebsitegenerator/theme-starter.git
cd swg-theme
```

## Install Dependencies

Install all required packages.

```bash
npm install
```

## Start the Development Server

Launch Astro in development mode.

```bash
npm run dev
```

Your theme is now available locally and automatically reloads whenever you save changes.

## Project Structure

The Theme Starter follows a simple structure.

```text
src/
├── components/
├── layouts/
├── templates/
├── lib/
└── styles/

export/
├── pages/
├── blog/
├── docs/
├── menus/
└── settings/
```

The `export` directory mirrors the JSON generated by Static Website Generator. Themes read these files directly.

## Build Your Theme

Customize layouts, components and templates to create your own design. The internal structure of your theme is entirely up to you as long as the required SWG templates are present.

## Validate Your Theme

Before packaging your work, validate the theme.

```bash
npm run validate
```

The validator confirms that your theme contains all required templates and configuration files.

## Package Your Theme

When your theme is complete, create a distributable package.

```bash
npm run package
```

The generated package can be imported directly into Static Website Generator.

## Next Steps

Continue with the following guides:

- Create Your First Theme
- CLI Reference
- Theme Validation

Happy building!
meta_description: Learn how to set up the Static Website Generator Theme Starter and build your first Astro theme.
